title: Cinematography basics
summary:
Cinematography (from Ancient Greek κίνημα ( kínēma ) ' movement ' and γράφειν ( gráphein ) ' to write, draw, paint, etc. ' ) is the art of motion picture (and more recently, electronic video camera ) photography.

Cinematographers use a lens to focus reflected light from objects into a real image that is transferred to some image sensor or light-sensitive material inside the movie camera . [ 1 ] T…
